segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The breathing of the Hall changed around false dawn. Not louder -- quicker. Someone turned over in a bedroll and muttered a name. The lichen dimmed, then steadied.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin woke from a dream that was only a pressure: Thud. Not near. Not far. Not a heart but something that had decided the rhythm of a heart was a good lie. Once, twice, and then nothing, as if it had leaned close to listen and then, disappointed, moved on.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He sat up, coin in his palm, and didn't practice again. The lattice hurt the way fresh bruises hurt. Useable. Unwise.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They rose before the Hall would have called it day. The fire-pit stank of cold ash and juniper resin; the chalk sigils were still bright but scuffed at two thresholds as if something had pressed there and reconsidered.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: In the far curve of the vaulted ceiling, a hairline crack ran through one of the load ribs -- fine as a drawn thread of ink, barely there, the kind of thing that meant nothing until it meant everything.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No one looked up.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin caught Elowen's eye across the Hall. She shook her head once -- not refusal, but honesty. She wouldn't ease his pain this morning. The cost was too high and the day too uncertain. He nodded back, and the conversation was finished without a word spoken.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They packed as quietly as seven people could. Straps pulled snug. Knots retied. The last of the water parceled and swallowed. Serana walked the perimeter and refreshed each ward with a careful ninth stroke. The chalk squeaked in the silence.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex checked her daggers with the same ritualistic precision she'd used since Thornwick -- each blade drawn, examined, returned. She caught Caelin watching.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "They're not going to sharpen themselves."

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "I know. I just -- "

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "If you say something sentimental, I will use one of these very sharp daggers to perform surgery on your personality. Consider that my emotional range for the morning."

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Understood."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik had his charcoal map rolled and stowed, the journal sealed in its oilcloth, every remaining tool accounted for.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He patted his tool-harness -- five charges, two lenses, one intact device -- and nodded to himself with the expression of a man who has counted what he has and decided to stop mourning what he hasn't.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan stood already packed at the edge of the Hall, precisely where he'd been all night, shadow flat and obedient at his boots. He looked tired in a way that had nothing to do with sleep.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When Serana passed him to check the last ward, she hesitated -- not at his shadow, but at his face. Whatever she saw there, she refreshed the chalk mark beside him with particular care.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: That was when the first sound came from the tunnels: a patient scrape like metal testing stone, answered a long moment later by a tapping from the sealed mining spur, and then the slow rhythm of water displaced where none should be moving.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Three sounds. Coordinated. Not random, not natural -- the cadence of something that had been listening to them rest and had decided the intermission was over.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's head came up.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Company,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, voice flat.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Positions,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana returned, already in motion.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "We move on Thornik's mark."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He lifted the lantern, checked the charcoal lines on the table one last time, and pointed toward the passage that would feed to the Forge Road.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Here,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Before the mountain decides for us."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They stepped into the breath of the Hall and felt it hold and release around them like a lung that knew their weight and had decided -- just this once -- to wait.
